title: Compute Aligned Training: Optimizing for Test Time Inference
new_code:
if labels is not None:
            x, y = logits[..., :-1, :].contiguous(), labels[..., 1:].contiguous()
            # Compute-Aligned Training: scale logits by temperature < 1 to align
            # training objective with best-of-N test-time inference (CAT, 2024)
            cat_temperature = 0.5
            loss = F.cross_entropy((x / cat_temperature).view(-1, x.size(-1)), y.view(-1), ignore_index=-100)
interpretation: The paper's core contribution is aligning training compute distribution with test-time inference patterns, meaning the model learns to allocate computational effort in a way that mirrors how it will actually be used during inference. However, MiniMind's extremely small architecture likely lacks the capacity and depth required to benefit from this compute reallocation strategy — the model is too constrained to meaningfully redistribute internal computation, so the modified training objective instead disrupts the basic language modeling signal, leading to worse perplexity.
